executiveSummary.text: Base case 55%: risk-on trend pauses rather than breaks. US futures are flat-to-lower after record-high closes, while crude is +~3% near $98 WTI after Trump rejected Iran's peace proposal. Confidence: medium because CPI/oil pass-through is the next test.

AI scarcity thesis remains intact but more crowded. Fresh Yahoo Finance coverage says investors are rotating from only GPU scarcity toward CPU, memory and broader bottleneck plays; this supports MU/semicap optionality but raises valuation-chase risk. Confidence: medium-high.

Rates are the swing variable: April CPI is expected to re-accelerate to 3.8% headline from 3.3% because of oil. Oil shock -> CPI -> fewer Fed cuts/higher real yields -> pressure on QQQ/AI multiples and REITs. Confidence: medium.

Gold is not a simple war hedge here. With 10Y real yield last published at 1.93% and oil-driven inflation threatening tighter policy, gold can fall even as geopolitical risk rises. Confidence: medium-high.

Singapore-specific verified fresh news is sparse over the last 24h; the read-through is macro: higher oil and sticky US yields are headwinds for REIT duration, while banks remain comparatively defensive through net-interest-income support. Confidence: medium.

regime.label: Late-cycle risk-on with oil/rates stress test

usGlobal.what: US stock futures slipped after Trump rejected Iran's proposal to end the conflict; Yahoo Finance updated the live market page at 7:01 AM SGT, May 11.
usGlobal.why: First order: oil rises and futures soften. Second order: higher energy lifts CPI expectations and reduces Fed-cut optionality. Third order: higher real yields compress long-duration AI/QQQ multiples even if AI earnings remain strong.
usGlobal.implication: Do not chase pre-CPI strength. Base case 55% is consolidation; deploy only on predefined drawdown levels rather than headlines.

usGlobal.what: Yahoo Finance's weekly preview says stocks closed at record highs Friday, with S&P +0.8% on the day and +2.4% for the week; Nasdaq +1.7% Friday and +4.5% for the week.
usGlobal.why: Momentum is being led by AI and semis, not broad cyclical acceleration. Second order: narrow leadership increases portfolio correlation inside KL's risk bucket; if CPI/yields disappoint, crowded winners can de-rate together.
usGlobal.implication: Keep core exposure, but avoid adding after vertical moves unless thesis-improving news arrives.

usGlobal.what: Fresh Yahoo Finance article: markets are chasing AI bottlenecks beyond GPUs, including agentic-AI CPU demand and memory scarcity; Micron, AMD, Intel and Korean memory names were highlighted.
usGlobal.why: First order: scarcity premium broadens from NVDA to memory/compute. Second order: equipment/foundry demand can persist if hyperscalers expand capex to relieve bottlenecks. Third order: the market may overpay for the bottleneck of the month, so earnings durability matters more than narrative.
usGlobal.implication: Supports KL's scarcity thesis; prefer semicap/foundry/memory exposure on weakness rather than chasing CPU headlines.

commodities.what: WTI crude traded near $98 and was up about 3% in Yahoo's market strip after the Iran peace proposal was rejected.
commodities.why: Oil shock -> higher headline CPI -> Fed cut probability falls / real yields stay firm -> pressure on QQQ, REITs and gold; banks can be relatively insulated if higher rates support margins but credit risk rises if growth slows.
commodities.implication: This is the most important macro transmission channel for KL today; do not treat it as automatically bullish for gold.

commodities.what: Gold was lower around $4,704/oz while oil rose; this divergence fits a real-rate-driven framework rather than a simplistic safe-haven framework.
commodities.why: If oil raises inflation and delays Fed easing, nominal yields and real yields can stay high. Second order: zero-yield gold becomes less attractive versus cash/T-bills. Third order: gold may only regain strength if growth breaks or real yields fall despite inflation.
commodities.implication: Maintain strategic metals allocation, but do not add purely because geopolitical risk is elevated.

narratives.title: AI bottleneck rotation broadens
narratives.description: GPU scarcity is no longer the only story; agentic AI workloads are raising attention on CPUs and memory. Scenario: 50% sustainable broadening, 30% crowded momentum overshoot, 20% capex digestion if yields rise.

narratives.title: Oil shock becomes rates shock
narratives.description: Iran-related oil gains are being watched through CPI, not just geopolitics. Oil up -> CPI up -> Fed patience -> real yields firm -> duration assets vulnerable.
narratives.source: Yahoo Finance live markets, May 11 2026 7:01 AM SGT; Yahoo weekly preview, May 10 2026 6:43 PM SGT
narratives.priceImpact: negative for duration; mixed for banks; conditional for gold

narratives.title: Record highs into inflation data
narratives.description: S&P/Nasdaq records plus incoming CPI create asymmetric near-term risk: good data can extend the melt-up, but bad data hits already-rich winners through discount rates.

secondOrderEffects.text: Oil rises because peace talks stall -> CPI prints hotter -> Fed cannot validate cuts -> real yields remain elevated -> QQQ/AI multiples and SG REIT valuations face discount-rate pressure.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Cash optionality at 26% is valuable; add risk only on valuation reset or CPI relief.

secondOrderEffects.text: AI CPU/memory demand rises -> hyperscalers may diversify capex beyond GPUs -> foundry/equipment/memory supply chains benefit -> but power/grid constraints become the next bottleneck.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Scarcity thesis should include semicap plus energy/grid exposure; monitor GRID/uranium and data-center power constraints.

secondOrderEffects.text: Gold falls despite geopolitical risk -> market is pricing real-rate pressure over fear -> if inflation stays sticky, cash can beat gold tactically despite gold's strategic role.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Hold metals as structural hedge; avoid adding until real yields roll over or price dislocates materially.

secondOrderEffects.text: Higher-for-longer rates support bank margins initially -> but if oil damages consumption/SMEs, credit costs rise later.
secondOrderEffects.implication: DBS/OCBC/UOB remain defensive income anchors, but monitor NPL language rather than just NIM.

investment.thesis: Accumulate AI scarcity infrastructure only on volatility, not headlines.
investment.trigger: Add in tranches if QQQ/SOX or core semis fall 5-8% on CPI/yield shock while capex guidance remains intact.
investment.whyNow: Narrative quality is strong but crowded; probability-weighted payoff improves after rate-driven multiple compression.
investment.invalidator: Hyperscaler capex cuts, margin pressure from overbuild, or order cancellations at ASML/AMAT/LRCX/KLAC.

investment.thesis: Keep Singapore dividends as ballast, but distinguish banks from REITs.
investment.trigger: Prefer DBS/OCBC/UOB on rate resilience; add REITs only if US 10Y real yield starts falling or REIT yields widen enough to compensate.
investment.whyNow: Oil/rates shock supports banks' relative positioning but hurts duration-sensitive REIT math.
investment.invalidator: Sharp credit deterioration for banks or sustained real-yield rise above ~2.1%.

trading.thesis: Do not chase Monday's AI winners before CPI.
trading.trigger: If CPI comes below expected 3.8% headline and yields fall, add tactical QQQ/semis; if CPI is hot and 10Y real yield rises, wait for a deeper reset.
trading.whyNow: This week's inflation print is the near-term regime switch.
trading.invalidator: Oil collapses on verified peace deal before CPI, reducing inflation-risk premium.

trading.thesis: Gold trade should be tied to real yields, not conflict headlines.
trading.trigger: Consider adding only if 10Y real yield falls below ~1.8% or gold sells off to a valuation/technical support zone while real yields stop rising.
trading.whyNow: Current setup is oil-up/gold-down, consistent with real-rate pressure.
trading.invalidator: Real yields continue higher or USD strengthens materially.

watchlist.thesis: Memory bottleneck is now the cleanest extension of AI scarcity.
watchlist.trigger: Watch MU pricing commentary and HBM supply/demand signals; add only on pullbacks after confirming margins are expanding.
watchlist.whyNow: Fresh coverage highlights memory as a bottleneck; crowdedness is rising.
watchlist.invalidator: HBM supply catches up faster than demand or hyperscalers pause orders.

watchlist.thesis: Energy/grid bottleneck remains the next derivative AI trade.
watchlist.trigger: Watch Constellation Energy earnings and data-center power contract commentary for confirmation.
watchlist.whyNow: AI compute growth transmits into electricity demand; oil/rates volatility may hide this structural signal.
watchlist.invalidator: Regulatory delays, power-price caps, or cancelled data-center builds.

riskWatch.name: Oil/CPI/Fed chain
riskWatch.description: Probability 35% that oil pressure pushes CPI/yields high enough to trigger a 5-10% correction in long-duration growth assets.
riskWatch.mitigation: Keep cash optionality; avoid leverage; deploy only at pre-set drawdown thresholds.

riskWatch.name: AI crowding
riskWatch.description: Probability 30% that bottleneck rotation becomes momentum chasing; semis can fall together if yields rise or capex optimism is questioned.
riskWatch.mitigation: Favor diversified exposure across foundry, equipment, memory and hyperscalers; size single-name adds cautiously.

riskWatch.name: Gold real-rate trap
riskWatch.description: Probability 40% that gold remains capped despite geopolitical risk if real yields stay firm.
riskWatch.mitigation: Keep strategic allocation but use real-yield confirmation before adding.

riskWatch.name: SG REIT duration risk
riskWatch.description: Higher US real yields transmit into SG REIT cap rates and refinancing assumptions.
riskWatch.mitigation: Prioritize balance-sheet quality and require wider spreads before adding Ascendas/CICT/MLT/FCT.

keyEvents.text: US April CPI Tuesday: Yahoo Finance cites consensus for headline CPI to rise to 3.8% from 3.3%, core to 2.7% from 2.6%.
Monday US earnings include Fox, Barrick Mining and Constellation Energy before market open; CEG is relevant to AI power/grid scarcity read-through.
Iran/oil headlines remain market-moving: rejected peace proposal lifted oil and softened futures in early Monday trading.

bottomLine.whatMatters: The next 48 hours are about whether oil shock becomes CPI shock becomes real-yield shock. That chain matters more for KL's portfolio than the headline geopolitical narrative itself.
bottomLine.whatToDo: Hold core AI scarcity and Singapore income exposures; keep cash ready; add only on rate-driven dislocations or CPI relief. Treat gold through real yields, not fear headlines.
bottomLine.whatToIgnore: Ignore stale weekend recaps and simplistic 'war = gold up' or 'AI headline = buy everything' narratives. The transmission channel is rates, capex durability and bottleneck economics.
